findings.text: University stated arrears of £4,605.55 on 8 April 2026.
University stated arrears of £5,526.66 on 20 April 2026.
Bank statement (Sep 2025–Apr 2026) shows no payments to Edinburgh Napier University or accommodation provider.
Arrears amount increased from £4,605.55 to £5,526.66 within 12 days without explanation in sources.

proposed_artifacts.content:
## Financial Review Summary

### Arrears Amounts
- 8 April 2026: £4,605.55 (NAP-SRC-0021)
- 20 April 2026: £5,526.66 (NAP-SRC-0048)
- Discrepancy of £921.11 unexplained.

### Payment History
- Bank statement (NAP-SRC-0012) from 01/09/2025 to 28/04/2026 shows no payments to Edinburgh Napier University or accommodation provider.
- Total outgoings: £19,057.59; total deposits: £19,057.88; balance: £1.20.
- High discretionary spending observed (e.g., Uber Eats, Klarna, retail).

### Key Communications
- 10 April: Anfal informed finance of meeting to discuss arrears (NAP-SRC-0049).
- 20 April: Anfal requested payment plan, stated inability to pay £5,526.66 in 5 days (NAP-SRC-0048).
- 21 April: Finance acknowledged different deadline (9 May) due to notice to quit (NAP-SRC-0048).

### Risks
- No evidence of payments made.
- Spending patterns may weaken hardship claim.
- Enforcement action possible if arrears not resolved.

### Next Steps
- Reconcile arrears figures with tenancy agreement.
- Obtain full bank statement and payment receipts.
- Verify any funding applications or support arrangements.
proposed_artifacts.title: Financial Review Summary

proposed_tasks.instructions: Cross-reference all arrears statements (NAP-SRC-0021, NAP-SRC-0048) with tenancy agreement and payment schedule to determine correct arrears. Request full bank statement and any payment receipts from Anfal. Identify any additional charges or corrections.
proposed_tasks.title: Reconcile arrears amounts and verify payment obligations

redaction_flags.text: Bank statement contains personal transactions and third-party names; redact before sharing beyond legal team.
Email addresses and phone numbers in sources may need redaction for privacy.

risk_flags.text: Arrears amount disputed or evolving; reliance on a single figure without reconciliation may undermine complaint accuracy.
Bank statement shows high discretionary spending (e.g., Uber Eats, Klarna) which could weaken hardship arguments.
No evidence of payment plan or funding application outcome; risk of enforcement action per notice to quit.

summary: Reviewed financial sources: arrears stated as £4,605.55 (8 Apr), £5,526.66 (20 Apr), and £5,526.66 (21 Apr). Bank statement shows no accommodation payments, low balance, and high spending on non-essentials. Contradiction in arrears amounts over time; no payment receipts found. Proposed follow-up to reconcile amounts and verify payment obligations.

uncertainties.text: Bank statement coverage may be incomplete; only excerpt provided, and no transactions explicitly labeled as rent or accommodation payments.
Reason for arrears increase from £4,605.55 to £5,526.66 not explained in sources; could be additional charges or correction.
No payment receipts or proof of payment provided by Anfal, despite university request.
